meta.title: MergeCore Go Rules
meta.description: Rules for production Go: errors, context, goroutines, channels, and stdlib idioms.
meta.notes: Gate with go: true. Assumes Go 1.21+.

scoring.normalisation: Per review unit (one .go file), sum penalties after optional quality_signal multiplier, then score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))).

interpretation.9_to_10: Errors wrapped, context threaded, goroutines bounded.
interpretation.7_to_8: Mergeable; tighten error messages or add context to a call site.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky: swallowed errors, missing defer, or goroutine leaks.
interpretation.0_to_4: Block: data races, unbounded goroutines, or nil-map writes.

rules.title: Discarded error with _ or bare return
rules.description: Assigning the error return to _ or ignoring it hides failures. Handle, wrap with fmt.Errorf("%w"), or return.

prefer: if err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("...: %w", err) }

rules.title: context.Background inside a request path
rules.description: Handlers and downstream calls should receive a ctx from the caller. Creating context.Background() mid-chain breaks cancellation and deadlines.

heuristics: Function signature accepts ctx but a fresh Background is used for an outbound call

rules.title: Goroutine launched without bounded lifecycle
rules.description: go f() without a WaitGroup, context, or channel tying its lifetime to the caller leaks work and complicates shutdown.

rules.title: Loop variable captured in closure
rules.description: Before Go 1.22 the loop variable was shared across iterations, a classic source of bugs. Even in newer versions, be explicit when capturing inside `go func(){}` or deferred calls.

prefer: pass as argument or re-declare inside the loop body

rules.title: Open resource without defer close
rules.description: os.Open, http.Client Do response body, sql.Rows, and other closables should be deferred closed immediately after the error check.

rules.title: Error compared with == instead of errors.Is
rules.description: Wrapped errors (%w) do not equal their sentinel with ==. Use errors.Is for sentinels and errors.As for typed errors.

rules.title: Interface defined on the producer side
rules.description: Idiomatic Go places interfaces at the consumer. Large interfaces in packages that implement them encourage unnecessary coupling.

heuristics: Package defines an interface and the only implementation in the same package

rules.title: Shared map written without synchronisation
rules.description: Concurrent map writes trigger a runtime fatal error. Guard with a mutex or use sync.Map with care.

heuristics: Map assignment inside a goroutine without sync.Mutex

rules.title: http.Client with no timeout in production
rules.description: The zero-value http.Client has no timeout. Use a configured client with Timeout or per-request context deadlines.

rules.title: SQL query built with string concatenation
rules.description: Concatenating user input into SQL is injection. Use parameterised queries via database/sql or a query builder.

rules.title: Strong pattern: wrapped error with context
rules.description: fmt.Errorf with %w and a meaningful prefix keeps the sentinel relationship and aids debugging.

rules.title: Panic used for ordinary error paths
rules.description: panic should signal programmer error or unrecoverable state. Return error values for expected failures.
